items.title: Train your own model: see whether it actually got better
items.text: The train dialog can now compare the result against an older model — the one you started from, the shared BubblR model, or any .pt file of your own. When training finishes, both are scored on the same pages and you get a verdict in plain words: better, worse, or no real change, with the numbers (mAP50-95, higher is better). Small differences are called noise rather than progress, so a run that changed nothing says so. Pick 'Don't compare' to skip the extra minute.

items.title: Model Trainer: Batch is -1 (auto) by default now
items.text: Batch defaulted to 16, which is a guess — and it runs out of GPU memory as soon as anything else is using the card (a wallpaper engine, a browser and a music player can easily hold half of it). -1 lets Ultralytics measure what is actually free and pick a size that fits. If your saved settings still carry the old default of 16 it is moved to -1 once; a number you picked yourself is left alone.

items.title: Fixed: training died with 'WinError 1455' after several minutes
items.text: On Windows every DataLoader worker is a separate process that loads PyTorch and CUDA again, committing hundreds of MB before it reads a single image. With 8 workers (Ultralytics' default), let alone 20, that runs the Windows paging file out of space and training dies — with an error that blames the paging file and never mentions workers. Workers are now capped at 4 on Windows by default, and if you set a higher number yourself the Model Trainer asks first instead of wasting your time. When it still happens, the error now says what to actually change. Verified on a real 237-page dataset that used to fail.

items.title: Train your own model, without the second app
items.text: Tools → Train your own model… trains a model on the pages you labelled, right inside BubblR Trainer. You give it a name and pick where it is saved — you get <folder>/<name>/weights/best.pt, and that file IS your model. Choose what it starts from (the shared BubblR model is usually best: it keeps what BubblR already knows and just adds your pages), how many rounds it runs, and the image size. Every field explains what it does in plain words. The log and a progress bar show what's happening, you can stop at any time, and one button makes AI-detect use your new model. The Model Trainer is still there for the full set of knobs.

items.title: Choose where BubblR looks for pages you already labelled
items.text: Ranking skips pages you have labelled before so you never do the same page twice. It used to check only your current dataset folder. Settings → Storage location now lets you add more folders — an older dataset, or a helper's export — and they are all checked together. Pages are matched by how they look, so a re-saved JPG or PNG under a different name is still recognised.

items.title: Install Ultralytics straight from Settings
items.text: Setting up the AI no longer means opening the Model Trainer. Settings → Experimental now has an 'Install Ultralytics' button right next to 'AI Python': it installs Ultralytics and PyTorch into the selected Python and shows pip's output live, so you can see it working. When it succeeds, that Python is remembered as your AI Python. Tools → Check AI setup… offers the same button whenever the packages are what's missing.

items.title: 'Install Ultralytics' actually installs it now
items.text: In the .exe build the Model Trainer used itself as the Python for pip, so 'Install Ultralytics' silently never installed anything — which is why AI detection and ranking kept failing on a fresh PC. It now always picks a real python.exe (and falls back to 'pip --user' when the Python sits in Program Files and can't be written to). The same fix applies to training, Test on image and baseline scoring.

items.title: AI errors finally say what went wrong
items.text: 'Ranking failed' and 'AI detection failed' used to throw the error away. Now a failed run shows a dialog with the real log plus a plain hint (missing Ultralytics/PyTorch/Pillow, missing VC++ redistributable, NumPy clash, corrupt model file). New: Tools → Check AI setup… lists the chosen Python, the ultralytics/torch/Pillow versions, GPU yes/no and the model file — with a button for whatever is missing. An AI Python that merely exists is no longer trusted: it is probed before use.

items.title: Panels remember whether you closed them
items.text: A docker you close (Tools, Boxes or Pages) now stays closed after a restart instead of reappearing every time — and reopening it from View → Dockers sticks too. Quitting the app no longer counts as 'closing' the panels.

items.title: A fresh clone can install and update itself
items.text: The two PyInstaller .spec files were excluded from the repository, so install.bat could not build the app on a new PC — which also forced you into the source version, where auto-update refused with 'Windows .exe only'. The specs are now part of the repo, together with a requirements.txt and a release workflow. The source version can also update itself now (git pull, from the start page).

items.title: Customizable keyboard shortcuts
items.text: Settings → Shortcuts lets you rebind every shortcut — the selection tools (rectangle/ellipse/polygon/lasso/wand), Set reading order, Auto order, and all the File/Edit/Page/View commands. Click a field, press your combo, or leave it empty to unbind. Each row has a Reset, plus a 'Reset all to defaults', and clashes are flagged in red. Defaults: R/E/P/L/W for the tools, O for reading order, Ctrl+R for auto order.

items.title: AI-detect & ranking work out of the box
items.text: AI-detect no longer needs a manually-configured AI folder: the app now scans your machine for a Python that has Ultralytics. Settings → Experimental (and the Model Trainer) gained a 'Find' button that auto-detects it and a 'Get Python' button that opens python.org — so you never have to hunt for python.exe.

items.title: Pages panel on the right, plus fixes
items.text: The Pages panel now defaults to the right, wide enough to show two thumbnail columns. Thumbnails no longer look squished when the panel sits along the bottom. The View → Dockers menu now actually shows/hides each panel, and the Settings window uses the dark title bar.

items.title: One-click installer
items.text: A new install.bat builds the app (if needed) and installs it per-user with Desktop and Start-menu shortcuts — no more 'build then run then make a shortcut'. It needs no admin rights, and if a finished build is present it installs that directly even without Python.

items.title: Ranking skips pages you already labelled
items.text: Rank && load no longer surfaces pages that are already in your dataset (exported). It matches them by image content (a hash that survives JPG/PNG re-encoding), so re-ranking a chapter only shows the pages you still need to do.

items.title: Ranking now scans subfolders
items.text: File → Rank && load now looks inside subfolders (e.g. chapter folders), so a folder whose pages live in sub-directories ranks correctly instead of finding nothing.

items.title: Fix: ranking a big folder said 'no ranked pages'
items.text: In-app ranking now reads the model's full output correctly. Before, a large folder produced a long result line that arrived split across reads and was parsed as empty, so ranking wrongly reported no pages. Fixed by buffering the output and parsing it once complete.

items.title: Fix: page ranking could get stuck
items.text: Ranking (File → Rank && load) no longer gets permanently stuck doing nothing if a rank job failed to start — it now clears the busy state and shows an error instead of silently ignoring further clicks.

items.title: Green button for new AI models
items.text: The start page now shows a green ‘Get new AI model’ button when a newer model is published to the BubblR-Model repo — just like the app-update button. It appears when the experimental AI features are on. Click it to download the latest model for ranking and AI-detect.

items.title: In-app AI ranking + choose the ranking model
items.text: File → Rank && load now ranks pages with the downloaded AI model right inside BubblR Trainer (no external tool needed) — pages with the most detections load first. Under Settings → Experimental you can choose which model (and Python env) to use, so the program works for other kinds of images too.

items.title: Shared AI model: pull from git & pre-label
items.text: The apps can now download the latest shared model from the BubblR-Model repo. In the Model Trainer: 'Download latest' uses it as a base to continue training. In BubblR Trainer (Tools, experimental): 'Download latest AI model' + 'AI-detect boxes on this page' auto-labels a page for you to fix up. Model Trainer also gained test-on-image, error hints, dataset checks, resume/patience, an advanced section, ETA/metrics and its own icon.

items.title: Model Trainer (experimental) + training guide
items.text: A companion app, BubblR Model Trainer, trains a YOLO model from your exported dataset (or continue-trains an existing .pt). Enable it under Settings → Experimental, then Tools → Train a model…. Read Help → Model training guide for what you need. The window title bars are now dark to match the theme.

items.title: COCO JSON export
items.text: Turn on 'Also write COCO JSON' (Settings → Storage) to save annotations in COCO format next to the YOLO files — for Detectron2, MMDetection and other frameworks.

items.title: Polygon tool & segmentation export
items.text: New click-to-add-point Polygon tool, plus YOLO-seg polygon export so you can train instance segmentation, not just boxes.

items.title: Updates tab & seamless auto-update
items.text: Settings → Updates: choose Automatic or Manual, install a specific version to roll back, and see your current version. Updates install themselves on next start with no restart prompt.
